surface.name: Descope Flows Quickstart through a tested sign-up or sign-in
surface.selection_basis: Under the workshop policy, this is the fastest commonly used documented route: the official guide recommends Descope Flows as the easiest integration approach, and the React quickstart provides a concrete native path. Account creation is treated as one opaque step at the official Console URL; only documented post-login actions are reconstructed.

alternatives_considered.name: Hosted Application with Descope Flows
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The guide presents Hosted Application and Native Implementation as two Flow-based options without selecting one for every developer.

alternatives_considered.name: Client SDKs without Flows
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The guide recommends Flows as easiest and places Client SDKs under Not Using Flows for developers who build their own frontend login experience.

alternatives_considered.name: Backend SDKs without Flows
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This peer alternative requires the developer to own frontend and session behavior, while the guide recommends Flows.

alternatives_considered.name: React Flow integration
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: React is one of many web, mobile, full-stack, backend, library, and generic choices. It is preserved as a downstream candidate outline, not asserted as universal.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Descope account and project, embed the recommended Descope Flow in an application, and test a sign-up or sign-in until the SDK reports an authenticated user and session.
assumed_prior_state: No Descope account, project, Project ID, configured Flow, SDK, application integration, user, or authenticated session is assumed.
The developer has a local application environment appropriate to the framework they select and can complete the end-user authentication method contained in the project's sign-up-or-in Flow.
No account signup, onboarding wizard, Flow modification, package installation, authentication, user creation, or session issuance was performed during research.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A React app renders the Descope `sign-up-or-in` Flow and completes a test sign-up or sign-in, producing an authenticated user and session.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The `onSuccess` callback reports the user, the session hook reports an authenticated state, and the React quickstart checkpoint can be marked `Application is working`.
documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The React quickstart identifies this checkpoint immediately after the first working authentication test. Backend session validation and customization are later work.

prerequisites.requirement: A Descope account and Console access.
A Descope project with a Project ID and a `sign-up-or-in` Flow available to embed.
A supported application framework and package manager for the selected quickstart; the documented candidate continuation uses React and `@descope/react-sdk`.

primary_path.action: Open Descope's Getting Started Quickstart Guide and select the recommended Descope Flows approach.
details: Flows are described as the easiest, recommended, straightforward, and extensible approach.
primary_path.output: Flow-based implementation choices and framework quickstarts are available.
primary_path.success_signal: The guide identifies Native Implementation and Hosted Application as Flow-based paths.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No navigation recovery behavior is documented.

primary_path.action: Choose Native Implementation or Hosted Application and select the application's framework route.
details: The Getting Started navigation includes full-stack, web, mobile, backend, library, generic, and web-platform options.
primary_path.output: A framework-specific or hosted Flow integration guide.
primary_path.success_signal: No single implementation and framework combination is selected for all developers.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Installation, embedding, session handling, and terminal details vary by selection.

primary_path.action: Sign up for or sign in to Descope.
details: Treat signup as one opaque workshop-policy step; do not infer form fields.
primary_path.success_signal: The Console project settings are accessible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Use the Console's displayed account recovery path.

primary_path.action: Open the project settings and copy the Project ID for the project containing the pre-created `sign-up-or-in` Flow.
details: The onboarding wizard pre-creates Flows; the React guide uses `flowId="sign-up-or-in"`.
primary_path.output: Project ID and Flow ID available for the app.
primary_path.success_signal: The Project ID is copied and the Flow is available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Create or select a project and verify the Flow in the Console.

details: Install the `@descope/react-sdk` package in the React application.

primary_path.action: Wrap the app with `AuthProvider` and render the Descope component.
details: Pass the copied Project ID to `AuthProvider`.
Render the Descope component with `flowId="sign-up-or-in"` and the documented success and error callbacks.
Use `useSession` and `useUser` to display authenticated content.
primary_path.output: A React authentication UI backed by the Descope Flow.
primary_path.success_signal: The app compiles and displays the Flow.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The error callback reports Flow or configuration failures.

primary_path.action: Start the React app and complete a test sign-up or sign-in through the embedded Flow.
primary_path.output: An authenticated Descope user and session.
primary_path.success_signal: The success callback reports the user and authenticated UI is visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Use the Flow error callback and browser console to diagnose failure.

primary_path.action: Mark the React quickstart checkpoint as complete.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Repeat the test after correcting the reported integration error.

branches.path: Embed the sign-up-or-in Flow using `@descope/react-sdk`, AuthProvider, and the Descope component, then test sign-up or sign-in until the app reports an authenticated user.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Requires a local React application and package installation; the terminal is observed through component callbacks and session hooks.

branches.path: Use Descope's hosted Flow application instead of embedding the Flow natively.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes domain, application, and integration steps while retaining a Flow-based authentication terminal.

branches.path: Use Client SDKs for custom frontend screens with Descope session management, or Backend SDKs for a developer-owned frontend and custom backend authentication APIs.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces the recommended Flow embedding route with materially different UI, API, and session work.

branches.condition: Building a B2B app that requires SAML or OIDC SSO
branches.path: Use the dedicated SSO quickstart to configure a connection, add SSO, and test with an identity provider or mock IdP.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Introduces tenant and SSO connection configuration before the authentication terminal.

friction_gates.description: The recommended Flow route still requires Native versus Hosted implementation and framework choices that change required actions.
A Descope account and project are required before a Project ID and Flow can be used.
The public Console shell does not expose signup, verification, onboarding-wizard, project, or Project ID convergence states.
The code route depends on an existing project and sign-up-or-in Flow before the SDK can show an authentication experience.

sources.title: Quickstart Guide
sections_used: How do you plan to use Descope?
Building a B2B app with SSO?

sources.title: React Quickstart
sections_used: Import and Wrap Application with AuthProvider
Import Descope Functions and Add Flows Component
Utilize the React SDK Hooks and Functions
evidence_supported: Project ID and Flow ID use

sources.title: Descope Console
evidence_supported: Absence of public account and project states

sources.title: Create and customize your entire user journey in a workflow
evidence_supported: Onboarding wizard pre-creates Flows after questions

excluded_after_success: No complete end-to-end success is claimed while the Console account and project transition remains hidden.

Backend session validation, production token handling, custom domains, Flow customization, branding, additional authentication methods, SSO, authorization, MFA, fraud controls, and production deployment occur after the first working sign-up or sign-in.
